ruleSummary: Every new PM-visible slice must declare measured performance evidence, a candidate/reporting-only phase, or an explicit non-gating rationale. Estimated, missing, derived, or diagnostic-only metrics must not be counted as measured gates.

minimumContract.appliesTo: New PM-visible support, refusal, runtime, cache, and release-readiness slices.

requires: host, OS/architecture, JDK, backend, and adapter where applicable
median and p95 or equivalent row metric
missing or estimated metrics fail the selected lane
median and p95 or frame summary
non-claim that this is not a release gate
reason why the slice is visible but not a gate
non-claim for broad observed runtime telemetry
rationale explaining why performance is not applicable to the refused support claim

forbidden: promoting reporting-only rows to release-blocking without selected policy and negative fixture evidence
counting derived selected-scene cache ledgers as observed WebGPU runtime cache telemetry
adding a new release-blocking threshold from KAN-020

sliceTypeMinimums.minimum: Link an existing selected release gate, add a measured candidate/reporting payload, or state a non-gating performance rationale. Support evidence alone is not a performance measurement.
Expose stable refusal/fallback reason and state that no performance gate is required because no support path is claimed.
Provide frame telemetry with warmup/measured samples and host/backend metadata, or mark the lane reporting-only with quarantine rationale.
Classify counters as observed, observed-partial, derived, or unavailable. Derived ledgers remain reporting-only until observed runtime cache telemetry is present.
Reference the selected gate matrix and preserve the distinction between release-blocking gates, candidate gates, reporting-only evidence, and non-gating refusals.

sourceEvidence.rationale: M59 is the already selected release-blocking performance target. KAN-020 does not create new release-blocking rows.
M67 serializes measured candidate frame rows and keeps them non-release-blocking.
Feature families without isolated timing payloads remain reporting-only.
M84 contains native timing samples but remains candidate/reporting-only until variance, adapter eligibility, and gate ownership are accepted.
M85 makes selected-scene resource/cache behavior auditable as a derived ledger, not observed broad WebGPU cache telemetry.

validationRows.assertion: The policy defines a required performance classification for every future PM-visible slice.
KAN-020 does not add a new release-blocking threshold or readiness movement.
M67 measured candidate rows and reporting-only families remain distinct.
M84 frame.kadre-windowed timing remains candidate/reporting-only and not counted as a measured gate.
M85 derived resource/cache ledger remains non-gating and is not observed runtime cache telemetry.

nonClaims: KAN-020 does not create a new release-blocking performance threshold.
KAN-020 does not promote frame.kadre-windowed to release-grade FPS.
KAN-020 does not count derived M85 cache ledgers as observed runtime cache telemetry.
KAN-020 does not count estimated or missing metrics as measured evidence.
KAN-020 does not claim broad Skia performance parity.
